memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir Herbert Herries Baron Herries  1370-1440
memoryArtifacts.description: Sir Herbert Herries was appointed one of the hostages of King James I in 1423. He was one of the lords that sat at the trial of Murdock, Duke of Albany, and was one of the Scotch nobles sent to France …

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Herbert Herries third of Terregles於1380年出生在英國蘇格蘭度福利爾吉德布萊特。他與Lady Margaret Douglas結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1440年7月4日在蘇格蘭度福利爾吉德布萊特享年60歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Herries
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Norman, Scottish, English: locative name from Hérils in Trévières (Calvados). The name in England may have died out before the end of the medieval period, but survived into the modern period in Scotland.
2 English: variant of Herry with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Harris .

variants.name: Herry
meanings: Scots: Herry, to plunder, most properly to plunder birds' nests

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Herbert Herries third of Terregles was born in 1380, in Kirkcudbright, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. He married Lady Margaret Douglas. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 4 July 1440, in Kirkcudbright, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, at the age of 60, and was buried in Scotland.

Il est décédé le 4 juillet 1440, à Kirkcudbright, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Herbert Herries third of Terregles(은)는 1380년 영국 스코틀랜드 드프리실르 커kc드브라이트에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Margaret Douglas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1440년 7월 4일 스컷랜드 드프리실르 커크쿠드브라이트에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.
